question:
Solve the following percentages problem. Show your reasoning step by step.

James injured his ankle and decides to slowly start working back up to his previous running goals and then surpass them.  Before the injury, he was able to run 100 miles per week.  He wants to get up to 20% more than that total in 280 days and each week he will increase miles walked in the week by the same amount.  How many miles does he need to add per week?
response:
Okay, so James used to run 100 miles per week and wants to get to 20% more than that. I think 20% of 100 is 20, so he wants to reach 120 miles per week total. 

Now he has 280 days to get there, which is... let me see, 280 divided by 7 is 40 weeks. So he needs to go from 0 miles (I think he's starting from scratch after the injury?) up to 120 miles over 40 weeks, which means he needs to add 120 ÷ 40 = 3 miles per week each week.

Wait, or does he start at 100 miles already? I'm a bit confused about whether he's starting from zero or from 100...
